title: senecio digitalifolius
intro: Senecio digitalifolius, also known as Digitalis or Foxglove Plant, is a biennial or short-lived perennial plant native to Europe, with tall spikes of showy, bell-shaped flowers. The plant contains digoxin and digitoxin, cardiac glycosides with medicinal properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation, angina, and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Senecio digitalifolius include its ability to strengthen the heart, improve circulation, and reduce symptoms of heart failure, although it should only be used under medical supervision. Herbal preparations of Senecio digitalifolius typically involve drying the leaves and stems, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but due to its toxicity, it is crucial to consult with a medical professional before using it.
